title: Mand
description: Level 1 manding includes three types of response forms: speech, sign language, and PECS. Assessment of the child's ability to mand (request) desired items, actions, or activities to determine the operant level and where to start an intervention program.

title: Mand 1-M
description: Emits 2 words, signs, or PECS, but may require echoic, imitative, or pointing prompts, but no physical prompts (e.g., cracker, book). (E)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he responds when an adult provides an echoic prompt, such as 'say cookie' when a cookie is present, for 2 desired items or activities. For a child using sign language, give him 1 point if he responds when the adult provides an imitative prompt, or speaks the word (intraverbal prompt). For a child using a picture system, the adult may point to the target picture and verbally prompt the child to pick it up. Do not give the child any points if physical prompts are required for signing or selecting a picture or icon. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he only emits 1 mand.

title: Mand 2-M
description: Emits 4 different mands without prompts (except, What do you want?) the desired item can be present (e.g., music, Slinky, ball). (E)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he mands for 4 different reinforcers without the adult saying the target word (echoic prompt), giving an imitative or pointing prompt, or for a child using signs, speaking the word (intraverbal prompt). The desired item or activity can be present, as well as the verbal prompt, 'What do you want?' (or something similar). 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he emits 3 of these types of mands.

title: Mand 3-M
description: Generalizes 6 mands across 2 people, 2 settings, and 2 different examples of a reinforcer (e.g., mands bubbles from mom and dad, inside and outside, a red bottle and a blue bottle). (E)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he generalizes 6 mands across 2 people, 2 settings, and 2 examples of the reinforcer. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he generalizes to 3 mands across 2 people, 2 settings, and 2 examples of the reinforcer.

title: Mand 4-M
description: Spontaneously emits (no verbal prompts) 5 mands — the desired item can be present. (TO: 60 min.)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he spontaneously mands 5 times during a 1-hour observation (without, 'What do you want?' or similar prompts). 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he spontaneously mands 2 times during a 1-hour observation.

title: Mand 5-M
description: Emits 10 different mands without prompts (except, What do you want?) the desired item can be present. (e.g., apple, swing, car, juice). (E)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he mands for 10 different reinforcers without the adult saying the target word, giving an imitative or pointing prompt, or for a child using signs, speaking the word (intraverbal prompt). The desired item can be present, as well as the verbal prompt, 'What do you want?' (or something similar). Mands to remove aversives such as saying 'no' or 'mine' can also be counted. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he emits 8 different mands.

title: Tact
description: Tact - Level 1 Milestones

title: Tact 1-M: Tacts 2 reinforcing items
description: Objective: To determine if a particular nonverbal stimulus (e.g., a child’s mother) evokes the word “mama” (or an approximation). A child’s first tacts may also be part mand in that young children tend to tact things that are reinforcing to them, such as their parents, siblings, pets, favorite characters, toys, etc. It may be hard to tell if the response “mama” is a mand or a tact, but that’s okay because at this early stage the goal is to determine if the child discriminates between the nonverbal stimulus of his mother versus, for example, his father. If he calls everybody “mama” do not give him credit for this skill.
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he names 2 items without echoic prompts when tested (e.g., “Who’s that?” or “What’s that?”). 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he names 1 item without echoic prompts when tested, but do not give him 1/2 point if he calls everything by the same name.

title: Tact 2-M: Tacts 4 items (e.g., people, pets, characters, or other objects). (T)
description: Objective: To determine if the tact repertoire is growing, and if an adult can evoke tacts during testing. These tacts may also still be part mand at this point.
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he names 4 items without echoic prompts when tested. 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he names 3 items without echoic prompts when tested.

title: Tact 3-M: Tacts 6 non-reinforcing items (e.g., shoe, hat, spoon, car, cup, bed). (T)
description: Objective: To determine if the tacts are breaking free from motivation as a source of control, and that the tact repertoire is growing.
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he tacts 6 items without echoic prompts when tested. Do not give the child credit for responses that are also part mand (e.g., he says “book” because he sees and wants the book). 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he tacts 5 items.

title: Tact 4-M: Spontaneously tacts 2 different items
description: Objective: To determine if tacting is occurring without adult prompts to tact. Typically, children begin to tact items without prompts or contrived reinforcers, because tacting the item correctly becomes automatically reinforcing for the child (e.g., “Dora!”).
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he spontaneously tacts (no verbal prompts) 2 different items during an observation (no time limits). 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he spontaneously tacts 1 item during an observation.

title: Tact 5-M: Tacts 10 items (e.g., common objects, people, body parts, or pictures). (T)
description: Objective: To determine if the tact repertoire is growing.
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he tacts 10 items without echoic prompts when tested. 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he tacts 8 items.

title: Listener Responding
description: Listener Responding - Level 1 Milestones

title: Listener 1-M: Attends to speech sounds
description: Objective: To determine if speech sounds are discriminative stimuli (SDs) for attending to people. Also, to determine if the child discriminates between speech sounds and other sounds in his environment.
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he attends to speech sounds 5 times. 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he attends to speech sounds 2 times.

title: Listener 2-M: Responds to hearing his own name 5 times (e.g., looks at the speaker). (T)
description: Objective: To determine if a child discriminates between his own name and other verbal stimuli that he hears throughout the day. This is one of the most common forms of early listener understanding for a child, and occurs because of the frequent pairing of a child’s name with adult attention, physical contact, and delivery of other reinforcers.
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he attends to adults by making eye contact when his name is called on 5 separate trials, with no specified time limit. 1/2 point score: There is no 1/2 point score for this skill.

title: Listener 3-M: Looks at, touches, or points to the correct family member, pet, or other reinforcer when presented in an array of 2, for 5 different reinforcers (e.g., Where's Elmo? Where's mommy?). (E)
description: Objective: To determine if the child discriminates as a listener among verbal stimuli and associates those verbal stimuli with the matching nonverbal stimuli. Children learn to distinguish between their parents and strangers quite early in development, and the related verbal stimuli (i.e., “mama” and “dada”) are often in the first group of words that acquire differential stimulus control over listener behavior. Other reinforcing items such as a favorite pet, stuffed animal, a cartoon character, or toy may also help to establish early listener skills.
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he correctly identifies 5 different family members, pets, or other reinforcers when individually named by an adult. 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he correctly identifies 2 different reinforcing stimuli.

title: Listener 4-M: Performs 4 different motor actions on command without a visual prompt (e.g., Can you jump? Show me clapping). (T)
description: Objective: To determine if a child’s motor behavior is under an adult’s verbal stimulus control (no imitative prompts).

title: Listener 5-M
description: Selects the correct item from an array of 4, for 20 different objects or pictures (e.g., Show me cat. Touch shoe). (T)
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he correctly identifies 20 different items in an array of 4 on the first trial during testing. When giving credit for differentially looking at items to demonstrate listener skills, make sure there is an array from which to discriminate, and that the response is clearly directed to the target stimulus. 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he correctly identifies 15 different items in an array of 4 on the first trial during testing.

title: Visual Perceptual Skills and Matching-to-Sample (VP-MTS)
description: Visual Perceptual Skills and Matching-to-Sample (VP-MTS) - Level 1

title: VP-MTS 1-M
description: Visually tracks moving stimuli for 2 seconds, 5 times.(TO:30 min.)
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he visually tracks moving stimuli for 2 seconds, 5 times during the 30-minute observation. 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if tracks moving stimuli for 2 seconds, 2 times during the 30-minute observation.

title: VP-MTS 2-M
description: Grasps small objects with thumb, index finger, and middle finger (pincer grasp) 5 times. (O)
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he is successful with small motor eye-hand coordination activities, such as reaching for and grabbing small toys and other objects, 5 times during observation. 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he usually requires 2 or more attempts to successfully obtain small items in front of him.

title: VP-MTS 3-M
description: Visually attends to a toy or book for 30 seconds (not a self-stim item). (O)
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he demonstrates sustained attention to a specific, and possibly reinforcing, visual stimulus for 30 seconds. Do not give the child a point if it is always the same item, or other items that might be classified as self-stimulation for the child (e.g., a stick that the child stims with). 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he attends to visual stimuli for 15 seconds.

title: VP-MTS 4-M
description: Places 3 items in a container, stacks 3 blocks, or places 3 rings on a peg for 2 of these or similar activities. (E)
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he successfully and independently places 3 items in a container, stacks 3 blocks, places 3 rings on a peg, etc., for any 2 activities during observation or testing. 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he places 2 items in a container, stacks 2 blocks, places 2 rings on a peg, etc., for any single activity during observation or testing.

title: VP-MTS 5-M
description: Matches any 10 identical items (e.g., inset puzzles, toys, objects, or pictures). (E)
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he successfully matches 10 items. 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he matches 5 items.

title: Independent Play
description: Independent Play - Level 1

title: Independent Play 1-M
description: Manipulates and explores objects for 1 minute (e.g., looks at a toy, turns it over, presses buttons). (TO: 30 min.).
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he independently manipulates and explores objects for 1 minute during a 30-minute observation. 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he independently manipulates and explores objects for 30 seconds during a 30-minute observation.

title: Play
description: Play and Leisure Skills.

title: Play 2-M
description: Shows variation in play by independently interacting with 5 different items (e.g., plays with rings, then a ball, then a block). (TO: 30 min.)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he independently plays with 5 different items during a 30-minute observation. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he independently plays with 3 different items during a 30-minute observation.

title: Play 3-M
description: Demonstrates generalization by engaging in exploratory movement and playing with the toys in a novel environment for 2 minutes (e.g., in a new playroom). (TO: 30 min.)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he independently engages in exploratory movement and touching in a new or novel play area for 2 minutes during a 30-minute observation. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he independently engages in exploratory movement and touching in a new or novel play area for 1 minute during a 30-minute observation.

title: Play 4-M
description: Independently engages in movement play for 2 minutes (e.g., swinging, dancing, rocking, jumping, climbing). (TO: 30 min.)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he engages in movement play for 2 minutes during a 30-minute observation. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he engages in movement play for 1 minute during a 30-minute observation.

title: Play 5-M
description: Independently engages in cause-and-effect play for 2 minutes (e.g., dumping containers, playing with pop-up toys, pulling toys, etc.). (TO: 30 min.)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he independently engages in cause-and-effect play for 2 minutes during a 30-minute observation. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he independently engages in cause-and-effect play for 1 minute during a 30-minute observation.

title: Social Behavior and Social Play
description: Social Behavior and Social Play.

title: Social 1-M
description: Visually tracks and shows interest in people's movement at least 5 times. (TO: 30 min)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he visually tracks and shows interest in people's movement at least 5 times during a 30-minute observation. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he visually tracks and shows interest in people's movement at least 2 times during a 30-minute observation.

title: Social 2-M
description: Indicates that he wants to be held or physically played with 2 times (e.g., climbs up on his mom's lap). (TO: 60 min.)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he indicates that he wants to be held or physically played with 2 times during a 1-hour observation. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he indicates that he wants to be held or physically played with 1 time during a 1-hour observation.

title: Social 3-M
description: Spontaneously looks (glances) at other children 5 times. (TO: 30 min.)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he spontaneously looks (glances) at other children 5 times during a 30-minute observation. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he spontaneously looks (glances) at other children 2 times during a 30-minute observation.

title: Social 4-M
description: Spontaneously engages in parallel play by other children for a total of 2 minutes (e.g., sits in the sandbox near other children). (TO: 30 min.)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he spontaneously engages in parallel play by other children for a total of 2 minutes during a 30-minute observation. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he spontaneously engages in parallel play by other children for a total of 1 minute during a 30-minute observation.

title: Social 5-M
description: Spontaneously follows peers or imitates their motor behavior 2 times. (e.g., follows a peer into a playhouse). (TO: 30 min.)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he spontaneously follows peers or imitates their motor behavior 2 times during a 30-minute observation. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he spontaneously follows peers or imitates their motor behavior 1 time during a 30-minute observation.

title: MOTOR IMITATION — LEVEL 1
description: Assessment of a child's ability to imitate the gross motor behaviors, fine motor behaviors, and actions with objects modeled by others.

title: Motor Imitation 1-M
description: Imitates 2 gross motor movements when prompted with, Do this (e.g., Clapping, raising arms). (T)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he imitates 2 gross motor movements presented by an adult. Even if the responses are approximations, score them as correct. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he imitates only one motor action. Don’t give any points if the child always emits the same behavior, such as clapping.

title: Motor Imitation 2-M
description: Imitates 4 gross motor movements when prompted with, Do this. (T)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he imitates 4 gross motor movements presented by an adult. Even if the responses are approximations, score them as correct. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he imitates 3 motor actions.

title: Motor Imitation 3-M
description: Imitates 8 motor movements, 2 of which involve objects (e.g., shaking a maraca, tapping sticks together). (T)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he imitates 6 motor movements presented by an adult, and can imitate 2 adult motor behaviors involving objects (a total of 8 imitations). Even if the responses are approximations, score them as correct. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he imitates 6 behaviors of any type.

title: Motor Imitation 4-M
description: Spontaneously imitates the motor behaviors of others on 5 occasions. (O)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he spontaneously imitates the motor behaviors of others on 5 occasions. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he spontaneously imitates the motor behaviors of others on 2 occasions.

title: Motor Imitation 5-M
description: Imitates 20 motor movements of any type (e.g., fine motor, gross motor, imitation with objects). (T)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he imitates 20 motor behaviors of any type. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he imitates 15 motor behaviors of any type.

title: EARLY ECHOIC SKILLS ASSESSMENT (EESA)
description: Evaluates a child’s ability to repeat a speech model, including speech phonemes, syllable combinations, and intonation patterns typically acquired between birth and 30 months of age.

title: Echoic 1-M
description: Scores at least 2 on the EESA subtest.
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he scores 2 or more on the EESA subtest. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he scores 1 on the EESA subtest.

title: Echoic 2-M
description: Scores at least 5 on the EESA subtest.
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he scores 5 or more on the EESA subtest. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he scores 3 on the EESA subtest.

title: Echoic 3-M
description: Scores at least 10 on the EESA subtest.
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he scores 10 or more on the EESA subtest. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he scores 7 on the EESA.

title: Echoic 4-M
description: Scores at least 15 on the EESA subtest.
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he scores 15 or more on the EESA subtest. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he scores 12 on the EESA subtest.

title: Echoic 5-M
description: Scores at least 25 on the EESA subtest (at least 20 from group 1).
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he scores 25 or more on the EESA subtest, with at least 20 from group 1. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he scores 20 on the EESA subtest with at least 15 from group 1.

title: Spontaneous Vocal Behavior - Level 1
description: Assessment of a child's spontaneous emission of speech sounds without prompts.

title: Vocal 1-M
description: Spontaneously emits an average of 5 speech sounds each hour.
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he spontaneously emits an average of 5 speech sounds each hour. A time-sample type of data recording can be used to measure this behavior. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he spontaneously emits an average of 2 speech sounds each hour.

title: Vocal 2-M
description: Spontaneously emits 5 different sounds, averaging 10 total sounds each hour. (TO: 60 min.)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he spontaneously emits 5 different sounds, averaging 10 total sounds each hour. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he spontaneously emits 3 different sounds, averaging 10 total sounds each hour.

title: Vocal 3-M
description: Spontaneously emits 10 different sounds with varying intonations, averaging 25 total sounds each hour. (TO: 60 min.)
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he spontaneously emits 10 different sounds with varying intonations, averaging 25 total sounds each hour. 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he spontaneously emits 5 different sounds with varying intonations, averaging 25 total sounds each hour.

title: Vocal 4-M
description: Spontaneously emits 5 different whole word approximations.
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he spontaneously emits 5 word approximations during observation. 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he spontaneously emits 2 word approximations during observation.

title: Vocal 5-M
description: Spontaneously emits 15 different identifiable word approximations during a 1-hour observation.
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he spontaneously emits 15 different identifiable word approximations during a 1-hour observation. 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he spontaneously emits 8 different identifiable word approximations during a 1-hour observation.
